# XLB documentation

XLB is a commercially supported, XDP-native IPv4/TCP Layer 4 load balancer for Kubernetes,
bare-metal Linux hosts, and virtual machines. It is built for high-volume services that need lower
infrastructure cost, minimal maintenance, and tuning-free operation.

## Start here

- [Documentation overview](https://docs.runxlb.com/)
- [Kubernetes quick start](https://docs.runxlb.com/getting-started/kubernetes/)
- [Bare metal and Docker quick start](https://docs.runxlb.com/getting-started/quickstart/)
- [Deployment options](https://docs.runxlb.com/getting-started/installation/)
- [Architecture](https://docs.runxlb.com/architecture/)

## Configure and operate XLB

- [Configuration overview](https://docs.runxlb.com/configuration/)
- [Configuration reference](https://docs.runxlb.com/configuration/reference/)
- [Kubernetes deployment guide](https://docs.runxlb.com/deployment/kubernetes/)
- [Helm chart reference](https://docs.runxlb.com/deployment/helm/)
- [Admin console and status API](https://docs.runxlb.com/operations/admin-console/)
- [OpenTelemetry observability](https://docs.runxlb.com/operations/observability/)
- [Connections and upgrades](https://docs.runxlb.com/operations/connection-lifecycle/)
- [Troubleshooting](https://docs.runxlb.com/operations/troubleshooting/)

## Product and commercial information

- [XLB product site](https://runxlb.com/)
- [Evaluate XLB](https://runxlb.com/evaluate/)
